cot_content:
Okay, let's think about this. The question is about how to distinguish between the left and right earbuds without looking. The options are to tie a knot at the connecting end or the speaker end of the left one.

First, I need to recall how earbuds are typically used. Earbuds have two parts: the part that connects to the audio source (like a phone or MP3 player) and the part that goes into the ear (the speaker end). When you're using earbuds, you usually have one in each ear, and sometimes they can be hard to tell apart once they're out of the case.

If you tie a knot at the connecting end (option A), that would be the part that's connected to the wire leading to the device. When you're using the earbuds, you probably don't handle that part much once they're in your ears. So if you tie a knot there, it might not be as noticeable when you're trying to tell them apart when they're out.

Option B suggests tying a knot at the speaker end, which is the part that goes into the ear. If you do that, when you're not using the earbuds and they're lying around, you can feel the knot on one side and know which one is the left. This seems more practical because you can feel the knot on the part you handle when putting them in your ears.

But I should also think about how people usually identify left and right. Sometimes, earbuds are labeled with L and R, and some people might mark the left one in some way. If you're tying a knot to mark the left one, it makes sense to do it on the part you handle, which is the speaker end.

So I think the correct answer is B: Tie a small knot at the speaker end of the left hand one.
label: majority
